title: Save schema and migration hardening
summary: Protect old saves and review snapshots before alpha feedback creates more states.

lane_1_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 1 to persisted visit/location normalization rather than reopening broad route migration behavior.
main_scope: Add pure helpers in src/engine/save.ts for biomeVisits and lastBiomeId normalization.
Wire the helpers into normalizeSaveState() while preserving valid save behavior.
Add focused src/test/save.test.ts coverage for malformed localStorage-like biomeVisits and lastBiomeId values.
out_of_scope: route definition or routeV2Progress migration changes

lane_1_main_result.result: Persisted biomeVisits and lastBiomeId now normalize safely during save load.
behavior_notes: Finite visit counts are floored to safe integers.
Non-finite, non-numeric, empty-key, and non-record visit entries are ignored.
Non-empty string lastBiomeId values are preserved; invalid values fall back to beach.
critic_focus: Confirm valid saves and snapshot round-trips remain behavior-neutral.
Confirm the new test locks malformed localStorage-style visit and last-biome values without widening route migrations.
Confirm no save schema key, world-state version, route progress, support unlock, UI, or copy drift landed.

lane_1_critic_result.result: No blocker found; save normalization hardening is scoped and verified.
notes: Malformed biomeVisits and lastBiomeId values are now bounded before entering runtime state.
Route v2 progress, support unlocks, debug snapshots, storage key, world-state version, UI, and copy were not changed by this item.
The existing dirty SAVE_STORAGE_KEY export predates this item and is covered by snapshot-key tests.

lane_4_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 4 to route-facing runtime regressions for normalized mixed High Pass saves, without adding a new migration layer.
main_scope: Add focused src/test/field-requests.test.ts coverage for a malformed persisted High Pass ready save after normalizeSaveState().
Prove seasonCloseReturnPending keeps the normalized ready High Pass progress in the dormant locator state until the calm close beat clears.
Prove clearing seasonCloseReturnPending turns the same normalized save into the ready-to-file High Pass note while suppressing active outing, route marker, and replay labels.
Prove stale High Pass progress cannot rehydrate active route, outing, journal, marker, or replay state after treeline-high-pass is already completed.
out_of_scope: normalizeRouteV2Progress behavior changes unless the test exposes a real bug
High Pass route-definition, station page, map, support-choice, copy, or geometry changes
new route history, replay, planner, or migration systems
baseline_checks: npm test -- --run src/test/save.test.ts -t "Route v2 progress|Low Fell|Root Hollow|outing support"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "High Pass|legacy notebook-ready Root Hollow"

lane_4_main_result.result: Added test-only route-facing regression coverage for normalized mixed old High Pass saves.
behavior_notes: Malformed persisted High Pass ready saves are normalized before route-state assertions.
seasonCloseReturnPending keeps ready High Pass progress in the dormant locator state until the calm close beat clears.
Clearing the close flag turns the same save into ready-to-file High Pass while suppressing active outing, route marker, and replay labels.
Filed High Pass wins over stale ready progress and selected route-marker support, so active request, outing, journal, marker, and replay state stay suppressed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "mixed old High Pass|High Pass|legacy notebook-ready Root Hollow"
npm test -- --run src/test/save.test.ts -t "Route v2 progress|Low Fell|Root Hollow|outing support"
critic_focus: Confirm the implementation is test-only and did not change save normalization, route definitions, support behavior, station, map, copy, or geometry.
Confirm dormant, ready-to-file, and filed High Pass phase expectations match the current helper contract.
Confirm stale routeV2Progress is asserted as harmless after High Pass is filed rather than silently migrated away.

lane_4_critic_result.result: No blocker found; lane-4 old-save regression coverage is test-only and scoped.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/save.test.ts -t "Route v2 progress|Low Fell|Root Hollow|outing support"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts -t "mixed old High Pass|High Pass|legacy notebook-ready Root Hollow"
notes: The implementation changed lane-4 route-state tests, not save normalization or runtime route helpers.
Dormant, ready-to-file, and filed High Pass phase contracts are explicitly covered for normalized mixed old saves.
Stale treeline-high-pass routeV2Progress after completion is asserted as harmless runtime state rather than silently migrated away.
Parallel dirty lane-3 files are present in the shared tree and are unrelated to this lane-4 review.

lane_3_scout_refinement.result: Narrowed lane 3 to a test-only saved spatial entry-id stability guard for debug snapshots.
main_scope: Add a focused src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts regression that builds all debug snapshots and collects save-backed entry ids from discoveredEntries, sketchbook slots, routeV2Progress.landmarkEntryIds, and routeV2Progress.evidenceSlots.
Assert every collected entry id exists in the live biomeRegistry entry maps.
Assert discovered entry biomeIds still point to live biomes that contain that entry id, preserving shared-species sighting locality.
out_of_scope: save schema version or SAVE_STORAGE_KEY changes
route definitions, High Pass phase behavior, station, map, copy, facts, geometry, rendering, or migration behavior changes unless the focused guard exposes a real broken reference
browser proof unless the test exposes a real spatial/content drift
baseline_checks: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "debug save snapshots|High Pass|route-marker"
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave"
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "spatial entry ids|debug save snapshots|High Pass|route-marker"
npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave"

lane_3_main_result.result: Added a test-only debug snapshot guard for saved spatial entry-id stability.
behavior_notes: The new save-snapshot regression collects save-backed entry ids from discoveredEntries, sketchbook slots, routeV2Progress.landmarkEntryIds, and routeV2Progress.evidenceSlots.
Every collected entry id must exist in the live biomeRegistry entry maps.
Discovered entry biomeIds must still point to live biomes that contain that entry id.
scope_notes: Test-only for snapshot and content integrity.
No save normalization, save schema, migration, route definition, High Pass phase, station, map, copy, fact, geometry, rendering, or browser-proof changes.
verification: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "spatial entry ids|debug save snapshots|High Pass|route-marker"
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave"

lane_3_critic_result.result: No blocker found; the saved spatial entry-id guard is test-only, scoped, and verified.
verification: Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts -t "spatial entry ids|debug save snapshots|High Pass|route-marker"
Passed: npm test -- --run src/test/forest-biome.test.ts src/test/treeline-biome.test.ts -t "Root Hollow|root-hollow|High Pass|talus|stone|canopy|cave"
notes: The guard covers discovered-entry keys, discovered entryId values, sketchbook slots, route landmark ids, and route evidence-slot ids.
Discovered entry biomeIds are checked against the specific live biome entry maps, protecting shared-entry sighting locality.
No save normalization, schema, migration, route, station, map, copy, fact, geometry, rendering, browser-proof, or player-facing behavior changed.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: Representative old/new saves load cleanly and do not snap players backward in route or High Pass progression.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Audit save defaults, migration assumptions, localStorage parsing, and stale route progress behavior.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Identify content IDs and route/note IDs that must remain stable; document rename hazards.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Confirm spatial carrier/inspectable IDs used by saves remain stable through recent place edits.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Test support selection, routeV2Progress, completedFieldRequestIds, and High Pass phase with mixed old saves.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
